behaviors.description: Missing required key across file+env reports exact config path (e.g., `database.host`).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the loader’s merge+validation flow across two sources (YAML file and `TOOL_` env). A unit test of only the validator can miss path-mapping/flattening bugs introduced when wiring sources together.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp config file that omits `database.host`, clear related `TOOL_` env vars, run the real config loader startup path (without mocking parser/env reader), assert load fails and error text contains the exact missing path `database.host`.

behaviors.description: `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` changes where config is resolved (instead of `~/.config`).
behaviors.justification: This depends on env resolution plus filesystem path wiring. A pure unit test of a path helper can miss that the loader still reads/writes the default path in real composition.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp filesystem, set `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` to a temp dir, place config only at `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/tool/config.yaml`, ensure no config at `~/.config/tool/config.yaml`, run loader, assert it reads from the XDG location and does not require the home-default path.

behaviors.description: `TOOL_` environment variable overrides conflicting YAML key.
behaviors.justification: Real risk is precedence bugs in source-composition code, not single-function logic. Mocking one source can hide incorrect merge order in the integrated loader.
behaviors.test_description: Write config with `database.host: file-host`, set `TOOL_DATABASE_HOST=env-host`, run real loader merge path, assert resulting config value is `env-host`.

behaviors.description: YAML syntax error reports parser line number.
behaviors.justification: Line-number quality comes from the real YAML parser error object and error-formatting integration. Mocking parser errors can trivially pass while masking real mismatch/omission.
behaviors.test_description: Create malformed YAML (known bad line), run loader with real YAML parser, assert failure message includes the reported line number token and references the expected line.

behaviors.description: Missing config directory triggers directory creation and defaults-file write before continuing.
behaviors.justification: This is a filesystem side-effect behavior. Pure in-memory mocks can miss path, permission, and write-order issues in real local FS interactions.
behaviors.test_description: Start with a temp HOME/XDG location where config dir does not exist, run loader first-run path, assert directory is created, defaults file exists with expected baseline keys, and loader proceeds without fatal error.
